VitalDB .vital file track analysis: Case 0002 tracks are SNUADC/ECG_II, SNUADC/PLETH, Solar8000/HR, Solar8000/ST_V5 (NO ST_II), Solar8000/NIBP_SBP, Solar8000/NIBP_DBP, Solar8000/NIBP_MBP, Solar8000/PLETH_SPO2, Solar8000/VENT_TV, Primus/PEEP_MBAR, Solar8000/VENT_RR. Case 0001 and 0003 have ST_II, but case 0002 does NOT.

privileged_info_summary: Case 0002 (0002.vital) is the longest surgical recording at 15,740 seconds (4 hours 22 minutes), compared to case 0001 (11,542 seconds, ~3h 12min) and case 0003 (4,394 seconds, ~1h 13min). The data comes from the VitalDB open dataset (Seoul National University Hospital), a real intraoperative vital signs database. Case 0002 has 76 physiological tracks including ECG_II, PLETH, heart rate (HR), ST segment analysis (ST_V5 only, NOT ST_II), noninvasive blood pressure (NIBP with systolic, diastolic, and mean), pulse oximetry (SpO2), and ventilator parameters (tidal volume, respiratory rate, and PEEP monitoring). CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: The metadata claims case 0002 includes 'ST_II' track, but analysis of the actual .vital file confirms case 0002 does NOT have ST_II monitoring—only ST_V5 is present. Cases 0001 and 0003 both contain ST_II, but case 0002 notably lacks this track.
